_meta.note: Exactly one notes record per institution (25/25). Keyano College record now included (was missing in prior pass). Venue authority fields (venue_contact_recommended, venue_contact_reason, venue_authority_status, venue_source_url) and the vendor_finding object live here per the schema correction. venue_authority_status values: Confirmed | Institution-directed | Operational contact only | Unconfirmed | Not researched.

notes.working_notes: Largest grad volume in AB. Three convocation periods/year (June/Nov/Mar) at the Northern Alberta Jubilee Auditorium in Edmonton. Multiple sessions/day split by faculty. Best first contact = convocation@ualberta.ca (verified). Flowers are NOT on the prohibited-items list (only signs/flags/banners), which is a positive signal vs U of Calgary. Augustana (Camrose) holds its own ceremony - treat as a possible separate operational plan.
notes.research_notes: Verified this pass from the official ceremony-details page: convocation@ualberta.ca is published for accessibility/seating. Spring 2026 dates June 4, 9-12, 15-17. 340,000+ living alumni. Jubilee is a Province of Alberta-operated venue - venue rules (loading, power, table placement, on-site retail) will matter operationally but there is NO source showing the venue selects the flower vendor.
notes.venue_contact_reason: Operational only - Jubilee Auditorium controls loading/setup/on-site retail placement, useful AFTER institutional interest. Not a decision-maker for vendor selection.

vendor_finding.evidence_summary: Reviewed U of A convocation + memorabilia pages: photography = GradImages, memorabilia = Bookstore, NO flower vendor named and no Commencement Group link. Genuine flower opening (subject to Jubilee venue rules).
review_flags: No flower vendor found - strongest large-school flower opening. Confirm Jubilee retail rules.

notes.recommended_next_action: Email the convocation office to (a) ask who currently handles on-site flowers/gifts at Jubilee ceremonies, and (b) ask about the process to be considered as a managed flower/keepsake vendor. Keep it to a renewal-timing question, do not name any competitor.
notes.information_still_missing: Named convocation coordinator; current vendor (if any); whether the Jubilee imposes retail restrictions; per-year graduate count; whether Augustana runs a separate vendor decision.

notes.working_notes: VERIFIED: flowers and corsages are only barred from graduands CARRYING them DURING the ceremony at Jack Simpson Gymnasium (on the not-permitted list alongside bags, jackets, mementos). This is NOT a venue-wide ban and does NOT restrict guests. A reception follows the formal ceremony. So families/guests CAN buy flowers to give grads afterward - a real post-ceremony gifting opportunity. Contact: convinfo@ucalgary.ca (verified).
notes.research_notes: Verified this pass from the official Graduating Students page. Ceremony at Jack Simpson Gymnasium; regalia pickup at the Olympic Oval. Spring (June) + Fall (Nov 9-10 2026) + Feb conferral. Official photographer is Artona. No flower vendor named for guests/families - worth checking whether the Commencement Group operates here too.
notes.venue_contact_reason: On-campus, university-controlled venue - institution is the primary path.

vendor_finding.evidence_summary: Official Graduating Students page: graduands may not carry flowers/corsages WITH THEM during the ceremony. Not venue-wide; guests unrestricted; reception follows. Post-ceremony/guest gifting is viable.
review_flags: Do not broaden the restriction. Check separately whether a flower vendor (e.g. The Commencement Group) already serves guests/families.

notes.recommended_next_action: Email convinfo@ucalgary.ca framing a stand aimed at families/guests for post-ceremony gifting (respecting the in-ceremony restriction on graduands).
notes.information_still_missing: Whether any flower vendor already serves guests; who approves commercial vendors; grad volume; per-ceremony schedule.

notes.working_notes: Large Edmonton polytechnic. Convocation June 22-25, 2026 at the EDMONTON CONVENTION CENTRE. INCUMBENT FLOWER VENDOR = The Commencement Group (NAIT's own gifts page links 'Preorder flowers' to thecommencementgroup.com/nait). Jostens = rings, Smart Photography = photos. NAIT is NOT open for flowers head-on. Bloom Boys angle = complementary products the Commencement Group doesn't carry, or a renewal-timing play. Contact: events@nait.ca / 780-471-8409.
notes.research_notes: Verified this pass from NAIT's official Graduation Photos & Gifts page and contact pages. The Commencement Group holds the flower slot. Venue is the Edmonton Convention Centre (external), which may have its own retail/loading rules.
notes.venue_contact_reason: Operational only - Edmonton Convention Centre controls loading/setup/on-site retail placement. Useful after institutional interest, not a vendor-selection decision-maker.

vendor_finding.evidence_summary: NAIT's official Graduation Photos & Gifts page states flowers are available at the venue on Convocation day and links 'Preorder flowers' to thecommencementgroup.com/nait. First-party confirmation of the incumbent.
review_flags: Incumbent is entrenched. Pursue complementary products or renewal timing, not a direct flower slot.

notes.recommended_next_action: Do NOT pitch flowers head-on. Email events@nait.ca about complementary keepsake products the Commencement Group does not carry, and ask when the current vendor arrangement is reviewed.
notes.information_still_missing: When the Commencement Group arrangement renews; which office owns that contract (events vs registrar); grad volume; Edmonton Convention Centre retail rules.

notes.working_notes: Keyano College Theatre (on-campus), Fort McMurray. 3 days (June 3-5 2026), multiple ceremonies. No flower vendor found = opening, but remote (~4.5hr from Edmonton). Contact convocation@keyano.ca.
notes.research_notes: Identity reconciled to Alberta.ca. No contact/vendor research done this pass beyond a fallback main line.

vendor_finding.evidence_summary: No flower vendor on Keyano convocation pages.

notes.recommended_next_action: Low priority (remote). No vendor, so a fit if ever routing through Fort McMurray.
notes.information_still_missing: Everything beyond identity: contact, ceremony details, venue, vendor status, grad volume.

notes.working_notes: TOP OPENING. Lloydminster campus (Lloyd Gymnasium) runs its OWN convocation (~late May, e.g. May 29 2026), separate from Vermilion (~June 5). Flowers sold ONLY via the campus bookstore in-house ('fresh roses, while supplies last') - no pro vendor, no Commencement Group. Closest AB campus to Saskatoon = ideal first managed-stand pilot. Contact Kyla Graham kyla.graham@lakelandcollege.ca / 1-800-661-6490 x8441.
notes.research_notes: Verified from the official convocation page. Two campus-specific ceremonies. In-house bookstore rose sales confirm demand with no professional operator in place.
notes.venue_contact_reason: Campus gymnasiums, college-controlled - institution is the path.

vendor_finding.evidence_summary: Convocation page: bookstore sells fresh roses (singles/3/6/dozen) while supplies last. No external vendor.
review_flags: Confirm whether the college would outsource to a managed stand.

notes.recommended_next_action: Email Kyla Graham proposing a full managed flower/keepsake stand for the Lloydminster ceremony (upgrade from the bookstore's limited rose table).
notes.information_still_missing: Whether the college wants to outsource; Lloydminster grad volume; Kyla Graham's exact role.

notes.working_notes: Edmonton EXPO Centre. INCUMBENT = The Commencement Group (thecommencementgroup.com/NorQuest). Prior 'whitespace' guess WRONG. Contact convocation@norquest.ca. Complementary/renewal angle only.

vendor_finding.evidence_summary: Commencement Group NorQuest page confirms on-site flower sales/pickup.

notes.recommended_next_action: Complementary keepsakes or renewal timing; do not pitch flowers head-on.
notes.information_still_missing: Contact, venue, vendor status, ceremony scale.

notes.venue_contact_reason: Arts Commons / Jack Singer Concert Hall likely controls on-site retail - venue permission may be required regardless of institutional interest.

review_flags: Confirm whether Arts Commons controls retail before pitching the college.

notes.recommended_next_action: Email the grad team, and separately check Arts Commons vendor/retail rules for Jack Singer events.
notes.information_still_missing: Contact, venue, vendor status, ceremony scale.

notes.recommended_next_action: Call the Convocation Office to ask who currently runs the foyer flower table and whether it's open to a managed vendor.

vendor_finding.evidence_summary: AU memorabilia page: pre-order flowers/gifts from Athabasca-based florist Woodland + Wildflower Weddings; limited on-site.
